Rice Alone (Educational: Protein Baseline)
educational_note: Baseline for comparison with 'Rice and Beans (Educational: Complementary Protein)' — illustrates a limited amino-acid profile eaten alone.

name: Rice and Beans (Educational: Complementary Protein)
educational_note: Pairs with 'Rice Alone' — same staple, illustrating protein-quality improvement from a complementary legume.

name: Lentil Dhal Without Vitamin C (Educational: Iron Baseline)
educational_note: Baseline non-haem-iron meal with no notable vitamin C source, for comparison with the lemon-pepper variant.

name: Lentil Dhal With Lemon and Pepper (Educational: Enhanced Iron Absorption)
educational_note: Same base dhal as the baseline variant, with lemon juice and red pepper added as vitamin C sources — illustrates the enhanced non-haem-iron absorption tier modelled in bioavailability.py.

name: White Bread Meal (Educational: Lower Nutrient Density)
educational_note: Similar-energy comparison with the wholemeal variant below — illustrates that energy content alone doesn't indicate nutrient density.

name: Wholemeal Bread and Vegetable Meal (Educational: Higher Nutrient Density)
educational_note: Similar-energy comparison with the white-bread variant above — same approximate calories, more fibre/micronutrients.

name: Standard Beef Mince Meal (Educational: Protein Quantity)
educational_note: Baseline standard-fat mince meal for comparing raw protein quantity against absorbed-protein figures.

name: Lean Beef Mince Meal (Educational: Protein Quantity vs Quality)
educational_note: Same dish as the standard-fat variant with leaner mince — illustrates that swapping fat content changes protein density without changing amino-acid quality.
